speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Here burns my candle out; ay, here it dies,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Which whiles it lasted gave King Henry light.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: O Lancaster, I fear thy overthrow

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: More than my body’s parting with my soul!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My love and fear glued many friends to thee;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And now I fall, thy tough commixtures melts,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Impairing Henry, strength’ning misproud York;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And whither fly the gnats but to the sun?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And who shines now but Henry’s enemies?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: O Phoebus, hadst thou never given consent

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: That Phaëton should check thy fiery steeds,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Thy burning car never had scorched the Earth!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And Henry, hadst thou swayed as kings should do,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Or as thy father and his father did,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Giving no ground unto the house of York,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: They never then had sprung like summer flies;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I and ten thousand in this luckless realm

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Had left no mourning widows for our death,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And thou this day hadst kept thy chair in peace.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: For what doth cherish weeds but gentle air?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And what makes robbers bold but too much lenity?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Bootless are plaints, and cureless are my wounds;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: No way to fly, no strength to hold out flight.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The foe is merciless and will not pity,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: For at their hands I have deserved no pity.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The air hath got into my deadly wounds,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And much effuse of blood doth make me faint.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Come, York and Richard, Warwick and the rest.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I stabbed your fathers’ bosoms; split my breast.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now breathe we, lords. Good fortune bids us pause

speaker: EDWARD
text: And smooth the frowns of war with peaceful looks.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Some troops pursue the bloody-minded queen

speaker: EDWARD
text: That led calm Henry, though he were a king,

speaker: EDWARD
text: As doth a sail filled with a fretting gust

speaker: EDWARD
text: Command an argosy to stem the waves.

speaker: EDWARD
text: But think you, lords, that Clifford fled with them?

speaker: WARWICK
text: No, ’tis impossible he should escape,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For, though before his face I speak the words,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Your brother Richard marked him for the grave,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And wheresoe’er he is, he’s surely dead.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whose soul is that which takes her heavy leave?

speaker: RICHARD
text: A deadly groan, like life and death’s departing.

speaker: EDWARD
text: See who it is; and, now the battle’s ended,

speaker: EDWARD
text: If friend or foe, let him be gently used.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Revoke that doom of mercy, for ’tis Clifford,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who not contented that he lopped the branch

speaker: RICHARD
text: In hewing Rutland when his leaves put forth,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But set his murd’ring knife unto the root

speaker: RICHARD
text: From whence that tender spray did sweetly spring,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I mean our princely father, Duke of York.

speaker: WARWICK
text: From off the gates of York fetch down the head,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Your father’s head, which Clifford placèd there;

speaker: WARWICK
text: Instead whereof let this supply the room.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Measure for measure must be answerèd.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Bring forth that fatal screech owl to our house

speaker: EDWARD
text: That nothing sung but death to us and ours;

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now death shall stop his dismal threat’ning sound,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And his ill-boding tongue no more shall speak.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I think his understanding is bereft.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Speak, Clifford, dost thou know who speaks to thee?—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Dark cloudy death o’ershades his beams of life,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And he nor sees nor hears us what we say.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, would he did—and so, perhaps, he doth!

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis but his policy to counterfeit,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Because he would avoid such bitter taunts

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which in the time of death he gave our father.

speaker: GEORGE
text: If so thou think’st, vex him with eager words.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Clifford, ask mercy and obtain no grace.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Clifford, repent in bootless penitence.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Clifford, devise excuses for thy faults.

speaker: GEORGE
text: While we devise fell tortures for thy faults.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou didst love York, and I am son to York.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Thou pitied’st Rutland; I will pity thee.

speaker: GEORGE
text: Where’s Captain Margaret to fence you now?

speaker: WARWICK
text: They mock thee, Clifford; swear as thou wast wont.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, not an oath? Nay, then, the world goes hard

speaker: RICHARD
text: When Clifford cannot spare his friends an oath.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I know by that he’s dead; and, by my soul,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If this right hand would buy but two hours’ life

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I in all despite might rail at him,

speaker: RICHARD
text: This hand should chop it off, and with the issuing blood

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stifle the villain whose unstaunchèd thirst

speaker: RICHARD
text: York and young Rutland could not satisfy.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ay, but he’s dead. Off with the traitor’s head,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And rear it in the place your father’s stands.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And now to London with triumphant march,

speaker: WARWICK
text: There to be crownèd England’s royal king,

speaker: WARWICK
text: From whence shall Warwick cut the sea to France

speaker: WARWICK
text: And ask the Lady Bona for thy queen;

speaker: WARWICK
text: So shalt thou sinew both these lands together,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And having France thy friend, thou shalt not dread

speaker: WARWICK
text: The scattered foe that hopes to rise again;

speaker: WARWICK
text: For though they cannot greatly sting to hurt,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Yet look to have them buzz to offend thine ears.

speaker: WARWICK
text: First will I see the coronation,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And then to Brittany I’ll cross the sea

speaker: WARWICK
text: To effect this marriage, so it please my lord.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Even as thou wilt, sweet Warwick, let it be;

speaker: EDWARD
text: For in thy shoulder do I build my seat,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And never will I undertake the thing

speaker: EDWARD
text: Wherein thy counsel and consent is wanting.—

speaker: EDWARD
text: Richard, I will create thee Duke of Gloucester,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And George, of Clarence. Warwick as ourself

speaker: EDWARD
text: Shall do and undo as him pleaseth best.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let me be Duke of Clarence, George of Gloucester,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For Gloucester’s dukedom is too ominous.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Tut, that’s a foolish observation.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Richard, be Duke of Gloucester. Now to London,

speaker: WARWICK
text: To see these honors in possession.
